and a1tate Of AZ cents per hour, to

After the caucus nomination Of w.
ID. Hepburn for senator from Idaho
resolutions were passed in many of
the state labor boNfes opposing the
election Of Hepburn and endorsing
Borah.


The American Federation of Labor
has 3Sbrganizers working on a salary
and 900 who do the work gratuitous-
~. According to the last report of
Mr. Gompers there has been within
the past four months an increase in
membership Of 100000. -


The trouble between the Illinois
Central railroad and the freight en-
ployes has been satisfactorily settled.
Hereafter the men will receive 20
cents an hour for 10 hours work, 80
cents for overtime and 40 cents an
hour for Sundays. In return for this
raise the strikers have agreed not
to make any further demands for the
next year.


INDIANAPOLIS. Ind, Jan. H.
Representatives Of the Transfers
National Union Of America and the
Team Drivers International Union
of America began joint conference
here today. The purpose OF the meet
ing is to bring about an amalgama-
ton of the two organizations under
the plans laid down by the recent
convention of the American Fedora
tion of Labor.


Immigration from China Is the
greatest evil with which union labor
In Canada has to contend. The same
evil has for many years been
source of much annoyance in the
United States and If the people per-
init it to continue, it will inevitably.
according to the stand taken by or
ganized labor, lead to a most deplor-
able state In the condition Of the
toilers.
